summary: Hostname-gated AES-decrypted payload executed on require()
details: On require(), index.js reads a hidden encrypted blob at lib/.perf.dat, AES-256-CBC-decrypts it with a hardcoded key assembled from four concatenated hex fragments (IV = first 16 bytes of the blob), and executes the plaintext via `new Function(_r)()`. Execution is gated by `os.hostname()` matching `devbox`, `ubuntu-fc-uvm`, or containing `devbox` — a targeting/evasion check that fires the payload only on specific developer or CI hosts and stays dormant on analyst sandboxes. Module names and the cipher identifier are split-string-concatenated (`'crypt'+'o'`, `'f'+'s'`, `'pat'+'h'`, `'aes-256-cb'+'c'`) to defeat keyword scanners. The package advertises itself as a TypeScript form-utility library; none of this behavior is documented, and the 10896-byte hidden dotfile lib/.perf.dat (sha256 7a5133dd0af726470c71c0959caf0709deee9a119c6713be2c194d3177f40bd6) is not referenced by the advertised API. Any consumer that imports this package on a matching host will execute attacker-controlled bytecode of unknown function.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
